commit.message:
Fix issues from PR #285: gate auto-resend, TurnEnd idle fallback, improved permission detection (#305)

## Fixes issues from PR #285

### Changes
- **Gate auto-resend on successful tools** (fixes #298): Added
`SuccessfulToolCountThisTurn` to SessionState. Permission recovery skips
resend when tools already completed this turn, preventing re-execution
of side-effectful work. Shows `⟳ Resending: "..."` when resending, or
`Auto-resend skipped` when skipped.
- **TurnEnd→Idle fallback** (addresses #299): When AssistantTurnEndEvent
fires, schedule a 4s delayed CompleteResponse. Cancelled when
SessionIdleEvent arrives (normal path) or a new turn starts.
- **Improved permission denial detection** (improves #300): Extracted
IsPermissionDenialText() helper matching "Permission denied",
"denied-no-approval-rule", and "could not request permission".

### Issues Addressed
- Fixes #298
- Addresses #299 (workaround for SDK bug)
- Improves #300 (more robust detection)

### Verification
- Build: 0 errors
- 20/20 tests pass

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: mutable arrays, this binding, property descriptors, poly IC (#442)

* refactor: migrate JsValue::Array from Rc<Vec<JsValue>> to Rc<RefCell<Vec<JsValue>>>

Update all call sites across the codebase to use .borrow()/.borrow_mut()
for accessing the inner Vec through RefCell. Key changes:

- Replace direct Vec method calls (.iter(), .len(), .get(), .push(), etc.)
  with .borrow().method() or .borrow_mut().method()
- Replace (**items).clone() and (*items).clone() with items.borrow().clone()
- Replace JsValue::Array(Rc::new(...)) with JsValue::new_array(...)
- Replace items[idx] with items.borrow()[idx] in test code
- Fix slice indexing to use .borrow() before slicing
- Update test assertions to destructure JsValue::Array and compare inner
  Vec contents directly, since PartialEq uses Rc::ptr_eq (pointer identity)
- Update doctests to use the same destructure-and-compare pattern

Files modified: install_globals.rs, dispatch.rs, interpreter/mod.rs,
wasm.rs, intl.rs, json.rs, iterator.rs, map.rs, set.rs, typed_array.rs,
function.rs, promise.rs, regexp.rs, ffi/mod.rs, heap_snapshot.rs,
value.rs, snapshot/mod.rs, stator_ffi/lib.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: make JsValue::Array mutable with RefCell

Change Array from Rc<Vec<JsValue>> to Rc<RefCell<Vec<JsValue>>> to enable
in-place mutation required by JavaScript array semantics.

- Add JsValue::new_array() constructor helper
- Update all match arms and destructuring patterns with .borrow()/.borrow_mut()
- Implement mutating Array methods: push, pop, shift, unshift, splice,
  sort (with comparator), reverse, fill (with start/end)
- Add keyed_store support for Array index assignment (arr[i] = val)
- Array equality now uses Rc::ptr_eq (identity) matching JS reference semantics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: implement this binding in function calls

- Add dispatch_call_with_this() that sets 'this' in global_env
- Function.prototype.call/apply/bind now pass thisArg to callee
- CallProperty handler sets 'this' in callee frame's global_env
- this is resolved via LdaGlobal('this') in the bytecode

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: upgrade to polymorphic property load cache

- Replace mono_load_cache with poly_load_cache supporting up to 4 entries
  per feedback slot (monomorphic → polymorphic specialization)
- Cache now works for PlainObject, Array, and Function (not just PlainObject)
- Invalidate poly_load_cache entries on StaNamedProperty writes
- Clear poly_load_cache on tail call frame reuse

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: proper property descriptor support

- Object.defineProperty handles accessor descriptors (get/set)
- Object.defineProperty applies writable/enumerable/configurable attributes
- Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor returns actual property attributes
- Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor handles accessor properties
- Object.freeze sets all properties non-writable + non-configurable
- Object.seal sets all properties non-configurable
- Object.isFrozen/isSealed check actual attributes
- Add PropertyMap::set_writable/set_enumerable/set_configurable methods

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v0.16.18: Fix token validation and support token rotation during migrations

- Add validate_and_store_migration_token() to accept new tokens if validly signed
  Allows token refresh on source (Bricks) without breaking import endpoints on target (Etch)
- Switch import endpoints to use flexible token validation instead of strict matching
  Import endpoints now store new tokens when received, enabling seamless token rotation
- Fixes issue where payload stops arriving when source generates new migration key
  Previously: New token from source was rejected (not in Etch DB)
  Now: Valid tokens are accepted and stored, continuing migration without interruption
- One active token per migration (subsequent generations overwrite via update_option)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add operational runbooks for incident response and common procedures (#1269)

Create 7 runbooks in docs/runbooks/ (data-recovery.md already existed upstream):

- incident-response.md: Triage → Diagnose → Mitigate → Resolve → Post-mortem
- leader-election-failure.md: ClusterState debugging, stale lease handling, manual assignment
- disk-pressure.md: WAL compaction, log cleanup, backup cleanup, storage expansion
- deployment-rollback.md: Docker, Azure App Service, and git revert rollback procedures
- scaling.md: Capacity planning, vertical/horizontal scaling, rate limit tuning
- security-incident.md: Breach response, credential invalidation, hardening measures
- secrets-rotation.md: Encryption key, API key, OAuth, SSL, CI/CD credential rotation

Each runbook includes: Symptoms, Diagnosis commands, Resolution steps,
Prevention measures, and Escalation guidelines.

Fixes #1269

commit.message:
Fix zone analysis to account for teams switching ends

The Shots by Zone section now classifies shots as Offensive,
Neutral, or Defensive relative to each team, adjusting for the
end switch between periods. In P1/P3/OT the home team attacks
right; in P2 they attack left. A home team shot on the right
side in P1 is correctly classified as offensive, and a shot on
the right side in P2 is correctly classified as defensive.

Previously the zones were just Left/Center/Right based on raw
position, which didn't account for the side switch and produced
misleading results.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Post-merge improvements: Copilot fixes, dual auth, feature spec files (#10)

* Add two-layer structure: installable scaffold + autonomous worker runtime

- Add worker/ — Dockerfile (node:20-slim + Claude Code CLI + gh CLI),
  entrypoint.sh (cron job model: clone/pull → run Claude → exit),
  worker-instructions.md (built-in instructions, per-repo override supported),
  README.md (Docker Desktop + Kubernetes deployment reference)
- Add .github/workflows/build-worker.yml — builds and publishes worker
  image to GHCR when worker/ or scripts/ change on main
- Add scaffold/specs/ — template source files for the installable payload
  (separate from this repo's live spec content)
- Add scripts/generate-dist.sh — copies scaffold sources + commands into
  dist/ with auto-generated do-not-edit headers
- Add dist/ — generated scaffold payload committed for downstream consumption
- Add .gitignore — exclude .claude/settings.local.json
- Fill in specs/spec.md with current two-layer system state
- Add specs/scaffold.todo.md and specs/worker.todo.md (filed from issues #7/#8)
- Update README.md with "Running on autopilot" section
- Update CHANGELOG.md WIP entries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Switch TODO format from checkboxes to plain bullets; add proximity principle

- Replace `- [ ]` checkbox format with plain `- ` bullets across all todo files
  and the knock-out-todos command: remove Step 0 orphan scan, update grep
  pattern, atomic remove-and-promote flow replaces check-then-move
- Add PHILOSOPHY.md "Proximity shapes behavior" section using the checkbox
  incident as a concrete example
- Add /respec Update step 5: detect TODO format changes and offer migration
  with user approval; add corresponding Ideas item to spec.todo.md
- Remove orphaned [x] items from worker.todo.md (already in spec.md)
- Add CONTRIBUTING.md documenting scaffold source layout and dist/ workflow
- Regenerate dist/ to include all command and template updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address Copilot review: dedup, non-destructive copy, format fixes, version pin

- Fix bootstrap PR spam: use deterministic branch 'scaffold/bootstrap';
  check for open PR before creating, exit early if one exists
- Fix install mode: rsync --ignore-existing so existing repo files
  (e.g. .github/workflows/) are never overwritten; add rsync to Dockerfile
- Fix /intake Path 1: emit plain '- ' bullets, not '- [ ]' checkboxes
- Fix dep TODO template (scaffold/specs/deps/README.md): plain bullets
- Fix knock-out-todos wording: "unchecked TODOs" -> "open TODO items"
- Remove undocumented EXECUTION_MODE from Dockerfile comment and spec.md
- Pin @anthropic-ai/claude-code to 2.1.71 to reduce supply chain risk
- Regenerate dist/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Make branch-before-work explicit in worker instructions

The vague principle "open PRs rather than pushing to main" wasn't enough
— the agent needs to be told to create a branch before touching any files.
Add a "Before you start" section with an explicit git checkout command,
and update "On completion" to push the branch and open a PR.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix stale branch handling, CI trigger gap, and stale docs

- entrypoint.sh: after confirming no open PR, clean up any stale
  local/remote scaffold/bootstrap branch before recreating it, so
  repeated runs are idempotent even after a closed previous attempt
- build-worker.yml: add dist/** to trigger paths so image rebuilds
  when scaffold sources change and dist/ is regenerated
- worker/README.md, specs/spec.md: update stale references from
  scaffold/bootstrap-* (timestamped) to scaffold/bootstrap (deterministic)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use * bullets in Reminders so grep for ^- naturally skips them

Removes the need for the "skip ## Reminders" caveat in the grep
instruction — the formatting itself enforces the distinction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add scaffold.md and worker.md; trim spec.md to system overview

Each feature TODO file now has a corresponding current-state spec:
- specs/scaffold.md — Layer 1 (installed files, dist/ generation)
- specs/worker.md  — Layer 2 (container, modes, CI/CD)

specs/spec.md is now a brief system overview that links to both.
Reminders in scaffold.todo.md and worker.todo.md updated to point
to their respective spec files instead of the generic spec.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Updated changelog

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Support Claude Code subscription auth in addition to API key

ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is now optional. Two modes:
- Subscription: mount ~/.claude from host (-v ~/.claude:/root/.claude:ro),
  omit ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Uses OAuth credentials from `claude login`.
- API key: set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY as before (pay-per-token API).

entrypoint.sh detects which mode is in use and exits early with a
helpful message if neither credential source is present.

Updated worker/README.md, specs/worker.md, and specs/spec.md to
document both options with concrete docker run examples.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Sync intake.md source with Copilot suggestion applied to dist

Copilot's fix (adding '- ' prefix to the INTAKE.md bullet template)
landed in dist/ via GitHub's suggestion UI but wasn't applied to the
source .claude/commands/intake.md. Patch source and regenerate dist/
so they stay in sync.

commit.message:
feat: replace WalHeadMap with O(1) WalDirectIndex for WAL lookups (#1302)

Replace the binary-search-based WalHeadMap (16-shard sorted arrays, O(log n))
with WalDirectIndex — per-table contiguous ulong[] arrays indexed by recordId
for O(1) cache-friendly reads.

Architecture:
- ConcurrentDictionary<uint, TableSlot> for table lookup (~10-50 tables)
- Per-table TableSlot: contiguous ulong[] indexed directly by recordId
- Lock-free reads via Volatile.Read on array reference and elements
- Growth via lock + copy (power-of-2, capped at +1M per growth)
- Tombstoning: value 0 = deleted/empty (NullPtr)
- LiveCount tracked via Interlocked for snapshot enumeration

Same API surface (TryGetHead, SetHead, BatchSetHeads, BulkLoad, CopyArrays,
Count, Dispose) — drop-in replacement for WalHeadMap.

Fixes #1302

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add resilience - outbox pattern, bounded channels, dead-letter handler

Outbox Pattern:
- OutboxMessage entity persisted in same DB transaction as domain ops
- DomainEventInterceptor now writes to outbox (SavingChanges) instead of publishing directly
- OutboxProcessor polls every 1s, deserializes and publishes to channel (batch 50, max 5 retries)
- OutboxCleaner removes processed messages older than 3 days (hourly)
- OutboxDbContext + OutboxMessage mapped in all 5 module DbContexts via extension method

Bounded Channels:
- Pre-created bounded channels (capacity 500) with backpressure for all event types

Dead-Letter Handler:
- DeadLetterEventBusErrorHandler replaces DefaultEventBusErrorHandler
- Logs CRITICAL when events exhaust all retries for audit trail

Reflection Cache:
- Type name cache in DomainEventInterceptor (ConcurrentDictionary)
- MethodInfo cache in OutboxProcessor for TryWriteAsync generic dispatch

commit.message:
fix: info panel opens under sidebar instead of into content area (#312)

## Problem
Clicking the ℹ︎ button next to the session name in the expanded view
caused the info panel to partially open under the left sidebar pane.

## Root Cause
The `.info-panel` in `ExpandedSessionView.razor.css` used `right:
-0.5rem` positioning, which made the dropdown extend **leftward** from
the trigger. When the trigger was near the left edge of the content area
(right after the sidebar), the panel extended past the content boundary
and was clipped by `overflow: hidden` on `.expanded-card`, making it
appear to open under the sidebar.

## Fix
Changed `right: -0.5rem` → `left: 0` so the panel extends **rightward**
into the content area. This matches the existing pattern used by the
sidebar header's info panel in `SessionSidebar.razor.css`.

## Testing
- 5 new regression tests in `InfoPanelPositionTests.cs` verify the panel
uses left-anchored positioning
- All 2110 tests pass
- Mac Catalyst build succeeds

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: Include featured images without _wp_attached_file metadata in scoped migrations

Critical fix for incomplete media migrations when selected post types are specified.

Problem: When migrating specific post types, featured images were being filtered out
if they lacked '_wp_attached_file' metadata. This happened because:
1. get_media_ids_for_selected_post_types() correctly collected featured images
2. But get_media_files() applied _wp_attached_file filter to ALL media
3. Featured images from draft/private posts often lack this metadata
4. Result: Collected IDs were filtered out = featured images not migrated

Solution: Only apply _wp_attached_file filter when migration is unscoped (all media).
When scoped to specific post types, trust the explicit ID collection which includes
featured images and content-referenced media regardless of metadata.

This fixes the issue where users reported 'not all media migrated' - especially
featured images that were set through WordPress admin but not through Bricks/Gutenberg.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
